question_text: Explain why red light has a longer wavelength than blue light, and what this means for their frequencies.
correct_answer: Red light has a longer wavelength than blue light because different colours of light have different wavelengths in the visible spectrum. Since all light travels at the same speed, when wavelength increases, frequency must decrease. Therefore, red light has a lower frequency than blue light.
explanation: This question tests understanding of the relationship between wavelength and frequency (wave speed = frequency × wavelength). Since the speed of light is constant, a longer wavelength means lower frequency. Red light (~700 nm) has a longer wavelength than blue light (~450 nm), so red light must have a lower frequency.
